design_decisions.thmsmith_propose_topic: New flagship topic: additive parallel-trends DiD versus odds-ratio equi-confounding DiD for binary outcomes. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchor is Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson 2024 Universal Difference-in-Differences, which replaces additive parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding for noncontinuous outcomes; adjacent anchors are Sofer et al. negative-control DiD and standard additive DiD decompositions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite observed 2x2x2 table frontier characterizing exactly when the additive DiD ATT, the odds-ratio/equi-confounding ATT, and a corrected scale-transport ATT coincide or diverge. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a hand-computed binary-outcome witness family with the same observed pre/post margins where additive DiD is zero but odds-ratio DiD is nonzero (and conversely), a closed-form contamination term Delta_OR-add(P), and an iff condition for scale-invariant DiD equivalence. If the proposal reduces to a restatement of universal DiD, a logit algebra exercise, or a generic noncollapsibility observation without a corrected estimand map and non-nesting witness, pivot or stop early.

proposed_from.topic: New flagship topic: additive parallel-trends DiD versus odds-ratio equi-confounding DiD for binary outcomes. Pre-anchor check: closest published anchor is Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson 2024 Universal Difference-in-Differences, which replaces additive parallel trends with odds-ratio equi-confounding for noncontinuous outcomes; adjacent anchors are Sofer et al. negative-control DiD and standard additive DiD decompositions. Our theorem is not that because the focal object is a finite observed 2x2x2 table frontier characterizing exactly when the additive DiD ATT, the odds-ratio/equi-confounding ATT, and a corrected scale-transport ATT coincide or diverge. Require a concrete nonroutine object: a hand-computed binary-outcome witness family with the same observed pre/post margins where additive DiD is zero but odds-ratio DiD is nonzero (and conversely), a closed-form contamination term Delta_OR-add(P), and an iff condition for scale-invariant DiD equivalence. If the proposal reduces to a restatement of universal DiD, a logit algebra exercise, or a generic noncollapsibility observation without a corrected estimand map and non-nesting witness, pivot or stop early.
proposed_from.novelty_justification: {"repo_axis":"The closest in-catalogue prior remains eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1, which juxtaposed ordinary DiD, proximal DiD, Universal DiD, and non-nesting regimes but was rejected because it used moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law. Version 2 adds an explicit positive finite SWIG witness and keeps the object as a binary exact-ID table frontier, not a generic proximal bridge claim.","published_axis":"The closest published papers identify or estimate nonlinear/OREC DiD effects, but do not state the finite observed-table equality frontier Delta_OR-add(P)=0, the open-cell additive-zero versus OREC-zero witness family, or the link-family condition excluding the logit-only branch. Wooldridge2023 was added as the closer nonlinear-DiD comparator."}
seed_list: Construct the observed binary DiD OR-additive contamination term and prove the exact equality frontier for additive, OREC, and corrected scale-transport ATTs.
Build open-cell finite SWIG witness families with the same three untreated/pre-treatment margins where additive DiD is zero and OREC DiD is nonzero, and conversely.
Characterize scale-invariant binary DiD equivalence across smooth monotone links and separate the logit-only equality branch from genuine scale invariance.
Decompose Delta_OR-add(P) into odds-ratio noncollapsibility, confounding drift, and additive ATT drift in a finite binary table.
Give sharp finite-table bounds for ATT under joint bounded additive-trend and bounded OREC violations.
Calibrate binary negative-control DiD by deciding when additive bias equivalence, OR bias equivalence, or a scale-spanning set is identified.
Extend the additive-vs-OREC frontier to a covariate-stratified binary DiD table with a collapsibility correction.
Give an OREC analogue of additive staggered-DiD contamination for binary outcomes after aggregation to risk differences.
Produce a finite-table diagnostic for nonlinear logit DiD interaction estimates versus OREC risk-difference ATT.
Construct an exact sensitivity curve delta* at which additive and OREC binary DiD ATTs switch sign.

seed_details.one_line: Construct the observed binary DiD OR-additive contamination term and prove the exact equality frontier for additive, OREC, and corrected scale-transport ATTs.

anchor_paper.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson
anchor_paper.claim: UDiD replaces additive parallel trends with OREC for noncontinuous outcomes.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: replaces the parallel trends assumption with an odds ratio equi-confounding assumption

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Decompose-then-correct is stronger because Delta_OR-add(P) is an observed correction and its zero set is an iff frontier.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from ParkTchetgenTchetgen2022 because OREC already identifies the ATT.
seed_details.rebuttal: UDiD identifies under OREC but does not characterize equality and strict separation from additive transport on the observed finite table.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not ParkTchetgenTchetgen2022 because the focal object is Delta_OR-add(P) and its finite-table zero frontier.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: routine logit algebra yields a new observed contamination object, corrected ATT map, and non-nesting frontier.

adversarial_referee.instance: May look like logit algebra without a flagship focal object.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The focal object is Delta_OR-add(P) plus the iff zero frontier and corrected ATT map.

adversarial_referee.instance: UDiD may already imply the same equality condition implicitly.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: UDiD states OREC identification; no finite additive-OREC equality frontier is named in the gap payload.

adversarial_referee.instance: A single numerical table could be dismissed as hand-tuned.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 promotes the witness to open-cell semialgebraic families.

seed_details.one_line: Build open-cell finite SWIG witness families with the same three untreated/pre-treatment margins where additive DiD is zero and OREC DiD is nonzero, and conversely.

anchor_paper.author: Prior CausalSmith reviewer
anchor_paper.claim: Earlier DiD frontier evidence used moment rows rather than a full finite law.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: moment rows rather than a full finite observed/latent law

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Counterexample seed is stronger because it targets a named failed proposal and demands open-cell finite laws.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just a numerical witness and not publishable.
seed_details.rebuttal: The seed requires symbolic open-cell inequalities and two non-nesting directions under shared margins.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not eid_proximal_did_bridge_frontier_v1 because it replaces moment rows with full finite observed/latent laws.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the deliverable is an open witness class, not one hand-tuned table.

adversarial_referee.instance: Witness could sit on a collapsed treatment arm.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Positive-cell assumptions and open-neighborhood persistence are part of the statement.

adversarial_referee.instance: Witness alone may not define a corrected estimator.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Chosen draft pairs it with Delta_OR-add(P) and tau_C(P).

adversarial_referee.instance: Claims M8 but only displays one table.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Conjecture 1 states an open semialgebraic family; Exhibit 9.1 is only a sanity check.

seed_details.one_line: Characterize scale-invariant binary DiD equivalence across smooth monotone links and separate the logit-only equality branch from genuine scale invariance.

anchor_paper.author: Athey and Imbens
anchor_paper.claim: Changes-in-changes uses scale-invariant nonlinear DiD structure.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: The assumptions of the proposed model are invariant to the scaling of the outcome.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Boundary/regime shift is stronger because it isolates a logit-only equality branch hidden by additive-vs-OREC comparison.
seed_details.referee_objection: This follows from standard link-function invariance arguments.
seed_details.rebuttal: The proposed frontier distinguishes all-link equality from a special additive-logit coincidence branch in a finite binary table.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not AtheyImbens2006 because it characterizes finite binary link-transport equality, not CIC distributional identification.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS: the object is a scale-invariance frontier with an excluded logit-only branch.

adversarial_referee.instance: Necessity across link families may be underproved.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Kept as Conjecture 2 with a Taylor/function-equation proof route.

adversarial_referee.instance: CIC may already cover scale invariance.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: CIC is a structural distribution model, not an OREC-vs-additive finite-table condition.

seed_details.one_line: Decompose Delta_OR-add(P) into odds-ratio noncollapsibility, confounding drift, and additive ATT drift in a finite binary table.

anchor_paper.author: Pang, Kaufman, and Platt
anchor_paper.claim: Crude-vs-conditional OR differences decompose into noncollapsibility and confounding components.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: can be decomposed into the sum of a noncollapsibility effect and confounding bias

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Mechanism decomposition is plausible but weaker unless the components are uniquely observed in DiD.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is immediate from PangKaufmanPlatt2016 applied twice.
seed_details.rebuttal: The DiD object couples pre/post transport and target-scale ATT error, but uniqueness of components remains less sharp.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not PangKaufmanPlatt2016 because the target is a DiD transport error, not crude-vs-conditional OR contrast.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade to field-tier unless paired with the frontier object.

seed_details.one_line: Give sharp finite-table bounds for ATT under joint bounded additive-trend and bounded OREC violations.

anchor_paper.author: prior_proposal
anchor_paper.claim: Prior additive trend relaxation used support-function geometry.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the family identified set is \hat\theta_\Lambda-\mathcal B_{\Lambda,\mathrm{TV}}

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Sensitivity seed is stronger only if it gives a critical slack or support function, not interval intersection.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just intersecting two known sensitivity intervals.
seed_details.rebuttal: The Stage -1.1 gap notes OR constraints couple periods and arms, so product-interval intersection fails.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not pid_tv_pretrend_atet_v1 because it adds OR slack constraints and active-face coupling.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS if support-function formula is explicit; otherwise downgrades.

adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would need delta* sign-switch curve in draft.

adversarial_referee.instance: Same body might bound ATT and ATU.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: A stronger version should state the family jointly.

seed_details.one_line: Calibrate binary negative-control DiD by deciding when additive bias equivalence, OR bias equivalence, or a scale-spanning set is identified.

anchor_paper.author: Sofer, Richardson, Colicino, Schwartz, and Tchetgen Tchetgen
anchor_paper.claim: Standard DiD can be read as a negative-outcome-control design with equal additive bias.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: Standard DiD can be read as a negative-outcome-control design

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Negative-control bridge is stronger if it yields a finite-table criterion rather than scale advice.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine negative-control restatement of Sofer et al.
seed_details.rebuttal: The finite binary criterion chooses additive, OR, or partial-ID calibration based on observed scale conflict.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not SoferRichardsonColicinoSchwartzTchetgen2016 because it replaces additive bias equivalence with a scale decision rule.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only with an explicit criterion or identified set.

adversarial_referee.instance: Sofer targets negative controls, not OREC DiD.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: The seed would cite Sofer as consumer and Tchetgen 2014 as calibration comparator.

adversarial_referee.instance: May lack a focal bridge class.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Needs named scale-spanning set or bridge criterion.

adversarial_referee.instance: Recent NC-DiD papers may already solve it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Would require deeper scan before choosing.

seed_details.one_line: Extend the additive-vs-OREC frontier to a covariate-stratified binary DiD table with a collapsibility correction.

anchor_paper.author: Mood
anchor_paper.claim: Odds ratios cannot generally be compared across groups or time points.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: cannot compare log-odds ratios or odds ratios for similar models across groups

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: CATE/covariate extension is weak unless it reveals a new collapsibility correction.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just stratification plus standardization.
seed_details.rebuttal: A collapsibility correction could be new, but this is less sharp than the unconditional frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Give an OREC analogue of additive staggered-DiD contamination for binary outcomes after aggregation to risk differences.

anchor_paper.author: Goodman-Bacon
anchor_paper.claim: Additive staggered TWFE decomposes into weighted DiD comparisons with possible contamination.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: weighted sum of that treatment's effect, with possibly negative weights

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Extension to staggered designs is strong only if OREC aggregation creates a new sign or weight term.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is a routine nonlinear analogue of additive decomposition.
seed_details.rebuttal: May be flagship if a new OR aggregation term is computable, but too broad for this v1.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not GoodmanBacon2021 because it concerns OREC binary-risk aggregation, not linear TWFE weights.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: lands; downgrade unless estimator and rate are included.

seed_details.one_line: Produce a finite-table diagnostic for nonlinear logit DiD interaction estimates versus OREC risk-difference ATT.

anchor_paper.author: Puhani
anchor_paper.claim: The treatment effect and nonlinear DiD interaction coefficient are not the same object.
anchor_paper.verbatim_quote: the treatment effect is the cross difference of the observed outcome minus the cross difference

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Estimator diagnostic is weaker unless it identifies an exact equality frontier against OREC ATT.
seed_details.referee_objection: Puhani already explains nonlinear DiD interaction interpretation.
seed_details.rebuttal: The OREC risk-difference target is distinct, but the seed is a diagnostic corollary of the chosen frontier.

seed_details.one_line: Construct an exact sensitivity curve delta* at which additive and OREC binary DiD ATTs switch sign.

anchor_paper.author: Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson
anchor_paper.claim: OREC is offered as an alternative to parallel trends for bounded outcomes.

seed_details.anti_pattern_check: Critical-delta is strong if delta* is tied to observed curvature and not a free epsilon.
seed_details.referee_objection: This is just sensitivity analysis around two point estimates.
seed_details.rebuttal: A closed-form sign-switch delta* would be useful, but it is secondary to the exact zero frontier.
seed_details.strict_delta: This is not TchetgenTchetgenParkRichardson2024 because it studies local violations around both restrictions.
seed_details.routine_collapse_check: PASS only if delta* is explicit and bounded by observed table curvature.

adversarial_referee.instance: May show only sufficient sign stability.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Must identify the critical sign-switch value.

adversarial_referee.instance: DiD sensitivity papers may subsume it.
adversarial_referee.rebuttal_or_downgrade: Binary OREC-vs-additive scale sensitivity appears distinct in gaps.

proposed_from.literature_map: Athey and Imbens 2006 Econometrica give nonlinear DiD and scale-invariant changes-in-changes; Sofer et al. 2016 Statistical Science recast standard DiD as negative-outcome-control additive bias equivalence; Tchetgen Tchetgen 2014 AJE warns additive bias equivalence can fail across bounded scales; Park and Tchetgen Tchetgen 2022 arXiv and Tchetgen Tchetgen, Park, and Richardson 2024 Epidemiology introduce Universal DiD and OREC for noncontinuous outcomes; Puhani 2012 Economics Letters separates nonlinear DiD treatment effects from interaction/cross-difference objects; Mood 2010 and Pang, Kaufman, and Platt 2016 identify odds-ratio noncollapsibility as distinct from confounding; Goodman-Bacon 2021, de Chaisemartin and D'Haultfoeuille 2020, and Sun-Abraham 2021 give additive DiD decomposition anchors; the live gap is an exact finite binary table frontier for equality, strict separation, and corrected transport between additive and OREC DiD.

banked_reason: Stopped at D-0.5 after angle0 v2 remained field-tier under flagship target: Theorem 1 was routine Delta_OR-add=q_L-q_A algebra/definition-unfolding, Conjecture 2 needed Roth-Sant'Anna/Wooldridge anchors, and the flagship upgrade would require a new estimator/inference decision rule rather than another proposal revision.
